mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate increases (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 5.0 percent signal deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains above 5.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer spending strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales fails to predict positive 60-day returns in over 55 percent of observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds sharply following supply chain disruptions or inventory drawdowns, creating immediate short-term momentum in equity markets as capacity utilization improves.
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 50 percent during post-shock recovery periods, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with industrial production rebounds.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate expanding construction activity and increased household confidence, which stimulates related sectors and generates positive forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable unemployment rates demonstrates healthy labor market absorption, supporting sustained consumer spending and driving positive equity valuations.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when payroll growth exceeds 200k but unemployment rises, or interaction effect is insignificant in out-of-sample testing (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 210 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses forward equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 70 percent of instances when CPI exceeds 210, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge over a simple linear trend model.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect immediate operational capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative or zero correlation with industrial production growth in more than 55 percent of observations, or signal decays within 3 trading days.
